Watch the video “Marginal cost”

Make summary (100 words) of the video explaining the meaning of the term “Marginal cost”.

In economics, marginal cost is the cost of producing one additional unit of a good or service. It is calculated as the change in total cost divided by the change in the quantity produced. Marginal cost includes all costs that vary with the level of production, such as raw material and labor costs. Fixed costs such as rent or salaries are not counted as they remain the same regardless of the quantity produced. Knowing marginal cost is important for pricing decisions as it gives an idea of the additional cost of producing additional quantities of the product.

atch the video “Ownership”

Make summary (100 words) of the video explaining the meaning of the term “Marginal cost”.

The concept of ownership represents control, possession, or rights over a property, whether tangible or intangible. It can be acquired by purchasing it with money, winning it in a bet, or transferring it by exchanging it for other property. Ownership confers the right to economic benefits related to the property and also associated risks. Ownership can be private or collective and is determined by law and the legal system of the country. The process and mechanics of ownership can be complex and may involve differing rights, control, and responsibilities over different aspects of the property.
